page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-48 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The memory configuration consists of 48.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 1.0 GB of memory per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results place the n2-highcpu-48 in the average performance tier across most metrics. It achieves a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1410.49 and a multi-core score of 44617.16. Memory bandwidth tests show 2446.02 GB/sec for cached small blocks, dropping to 153.47 GB/sec for uncached large block reads. In application-specific tests, the server processes 41.27 million Redis SET operations per second and delivers a static web serving throughput of 29.33 GB/sec.

The primary tradeoff of the n2-highcpu-48 is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio. With only 1.0 GB of RAM per core, memory-bound workloads may experience bottlenecks, and LLM text generation on even small 135M models is relatively weak at 53.69 tokens/sec. However, for compute-heavy tasks that do not require large memory footprints, the server offers a cost-efficient allocation of dedicated CPU resources without the overhead of unused RAM or bundled local storage.

This server is configured for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, where it scores 30076.0 on the Geekbench Clang test, as well as ray tracing, image processing, and multi-threaded decompression. It is also recommended for static web serving and high-throughput database operations that fit within its 48 GB memory limit.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-48 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by a dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor. It is equipped with 48.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and contains no bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 44617.16 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 10859.0. The server demonstrates strong capabilities in software compilation and static web serving, achieving a throughput of 29.33 GB/sec. However, its low memory-to-CPU ratio presents a tradeoff for memory-intensive tasks, as evidenced by a weak text generation speed of 53.69 tokens/sec on small LLM models. This configuration is recommended for compute-heavy workloads such as multi-threaded decompression, software building, and web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-48 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 48 vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and a dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering balanced multi-core performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-48 is an x86_64 server with 48 vCPUs and 48 GB RAM, optimized for compute-intensive workloads without local storage.
tagline: A dedicated 48-vCPU compute-optimized instance featuring a one-to-one gigabyte-to-core ratio for CPU-heavy processing workloads without local storage overhead.

48.0 GB of RAM providing a lean 1.0 GB per vCPU ratio
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators included
Average multi-core performance with a stress-ng multi-core score of 44617.16
Weak text generation performance on small language models due to memory constraints
Recommended for software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, and web serving
